home.title: Home
home.aboutDescription: Backend Software Engineer at Mid-Level III, focused on fintech, payments, and mission-critical systems. I build and maintain backend services with an emphasis on reliability, traceability, observability, and 24/7 operations.
home.aboutIntro: I build, evolve, and operate backend software for environments where availability, traceability, and operational predictability directly impact the business.
home.aboutDetail: My experience spans account systems, payments, bank transfers, notifications, financial integrations, messaging, deployments, QA, monitoring, and infrastructure. I work with Elixir, Kotlin, Kubernetes, cloud-native practices, observability, and AI-powered automations, helping reduce manual work, improve incident diagnosis, and bring more predictability to operations.

aboutCards.title: Fintech and payments
aboutCards.description: Experience with accounts, transfers, notifications, payment methods, and critical integrations for financial operations.

aboutCards.title: Production reliability

aboutCards.title: Applied automation
aboutCards.description: I use AI, n8n, LLMs, and integrations to automate workflows, classify requests, and reduce manual steps in real processes.

home.experienceDescription: Experience in backend engineering, payments, integrations, automation, and operation of mission-critical production systems.

highlights: Worked on an accounts and payments platform processing thousands of transactions per day.
Developed and maintained critical financial transaction APIs, with a focus on traceability, reliability, and operational safety.
Evolved bank transfer flows, financial integrations, notifications, and asynchronous processing.
Instrumented metrics, logs, and alerts to improve visibility into queues, workers, webhooks, and production application behavior.
Participated in incident investigation and response in a 24/7 environment, connecting technical symptoms to operational impact.
Collaborated on architecture, messaging, and observability decisions to increase predictability and reduce bottlenecks in critical systems.
Developed internal systems, APIs, and integrations to support the company's operational processes.
Worked across infrastructure, on-premise servers, databases, deployments, and environment maintenance.
Built automations to reduce manual tasks and increase predictability in internal routines.
Maintained applications used by thousands of users, including technical support, monitoring, and production issue resolution.
Contributed to improvements in organization, reliability, and maintainability of legacy systems and local environments.
Worked on payment methods, integrations, APIs, and system maintenance in production environments.
Developed backend and frontend features, supporting product flows, maintenance, and feature evolution.
Participated in QA, deployments, monitoring, bug fixing, and production issue analysis.
Collaborated with product and engineering teams to deliver improvements in financial and operational workflows.
Gained hands-on experience with maintenance, traceability, and incremental evolution of existing systems.
Developed backend and frontend features for a real-time communication, bots, and automation platform.
Built a build system from scratch and supported deployments, QA, monitoring, and environment maintenance.
Integrated APIs, webhooks, on-premise servers, and external automations.
Worked on infrastructure, technical support, and maintenance of applications used by a high volume of users.
Used Elixir and concurrent processing to handle real-time message flows and integrations.

home.projectsDescription: Practical cases in critical systems, payments, messaging, observability, and AI automation, connecting technical decisions to operational impact.

projectCards.title: Intelligent Triage and Development Workflow Automation
projectCards.description: n8n • AI • Slack • Git Workflow Automation. Automation built to classify requests, integrate internal systems, and reduce manual steps across operational and development workflows.
projectCards.alt: Workflow diagram with connected nodes representing automation, integrations, and a development pipeline
projectCards.problem: The team continuously received helper requests, fixes, and refactors that had to be analyzed, classified, routed, and communicated manually before development could begin.
projectCards.solution: I built an automation with n8n integrated with AI and Slack to interpret context, classify requests, route each item to the correct flow, and start steps related to fix or refactor tasks.
projectCards.impact: The automation reduced manual steps in the triage flow and reinforced the importance of building observable, resilient automations aimed at real bottlenecks, using AI as support for operational decision-making.
projectCards.role: I developed the end-to-end flow, connecting AI classification, n8n orchestration, Slack notifications, and development workflow steps.

projectCards.title: Statement 2.0 • Swap
projectCards.description: Statement flow redesign with Kotlin and MongoDB to provide a more consistent balance history, using clear contracts and a data model shaped for financial queries.
projectCards.problem: BalanceHistory 1.0 depended on ETL, had poor synchronization, and often returned unrealistic data for statement and balance queries.
projectCards.solution: I refactored the Statement 2.0 flow in Kotlin, structured MongoDB around the PACELC method, defined clear contracts, and handled race conditions.
projectCards.impact: The new structure reduced balance-history inconsistencies, improved query reliability, and made the flow less dependent on fragile asynchronous processes.
projectCards.role: I worked on MongoDB modeling, service contract evolution, and concurrency concerns, while also joining MongoDB Atlas training to support the technical decisions.

projectCards.title: Webhooks and Notifications • Swap
projectCards.description: Messaging system for high-volume webhooks and notifications. The work brought visibility into the real message flow by quantity, URL, and realm, exposing bottlenecks that were previously invisible in the application.
projectCards.problem: The system processed a high volume of messages, but operations lacked clarity on which URLs, realms, and queues concentrated load, or how Oban worker priorities affected processing.
projectCards.solution: I instrumented telemetry to count messages by URL, realm, and application flow, connecting operational metrics to worker behavior and making visible where the queue was actually accumulating.
projectCards.impact: The diagnosis moved beyond intuition: it became possible to identify bottlenecks, adjust processing priorities, and document the incident in a technical article with the investigation and correction path.
projectCards.role: I instrumented telemetry, investigated Oban worker behavior, connected metrics to operational impact, and documented the diagnostic process in a technical article.

projectCards.title: In-Memory Cache to Reduce Database Load
projectCards.description: RememberMe • Elixir • ETS • Performance Engineering. Open-source in-memory cache library created to reduce repetitive queries and improve read performance in high-concurrency scenarios.
projectCards.problem: A service was receiving approximately 2,000 concurrent requests querying the same dataset. Repetitive access created unnecessary database load and increased application latency.
projectCards.solution: I developed RememberMe, an ETS-based in-memory caching solution to store frequently accessed data and avoid repeated database queries. The project was published as an open-source package on Hex for reuse across different applications.
projectCards.impact: The solution reduced pressure on the database, improved read-operation latency, and consolidated a reusable in-memory cache approach for Elixir applications in high-concurrency scenarios.
projectCards.role: I identified the performance bottleneck, defined the caching strategy, implemented the library, published the package on Hex, and documented its usage.

projectCards.title: Conversational Bots • Alertrack
projectCards.description: Conversational bot platform for real-time customer service and communication via WhatsApp, connecting messages, business rules, webhooks, and external automations.
projectCards.problem: Support more than 500 thousand active users sending messages simultaneously without turning conversation processing, integrations, and webhooks into an operational bottleneck.
projectCards.solution: I used Elixir for its lightweight process-based concurrency, modeling webhook ingestion, asynchronous processing, and heavy caching to keep the system responsive under load.
projectCards.impact: The architecture absorbed a high volume of real-time messages, reduced manual steps in customer service, and allowed automations to evolve without compromising operational stability.
projectCards.role: I worked on the backend and integrations, modeling webhook ingestion, concurrent processing, caching, and support for external automations.
